summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (identity_resolution_errors is infrastructure ERROR table, not new sub-hub canonical; outreach cantonal pattern intact), Registry-First (CTB pre-registration at Section 0 before CREATE TABLE), Sovereign ID (no minting, resolves existing identities), No sideways calls (reads outreach.outreach + cl.company_identity for resolution, writes only to outreach.identity_resolution_errors). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 6 acceptance criteria met. identity_resolution_errors created with ERROR semantics, append-only guard trigger (resolved_at-only updates), DELETE blocked. fn_resolve_identity with 4 resolution paths (outreach_id > sovereign_id > domain > ein), hard-fail on missing anchor/multiple matches/ambiguous bridge, strict JSON return {outreach_id, sovereign_id}, RETURN NULL on all error paths (correct — no RAISE-after-DML). identity_resolver role with EXECUTE-only grant, REVOKE ALL on canonical tables. 14 enforcement tests across 6 categories. Phase 2B satisfied by SRC2 implementation. OBSERVATIONS: (1) Error handling is correct — RETURN NULL after INSERT (no RAISE-after-DML risk). (2) Column name concern carried from SRC2: function queries outreach.outreach.sovereign_id but CLAUDE.md documents sovereign_company_id — requires schema verification before deployment. (3) Domain/EIN columns on outreach.outreach may not exist (typically on company_target and dol) — Path 3 and 4 require schema verification. (4) Satisfied-by pattern: CS explicitly references SRC2 same migration/test files.
